blockers: Lot B has a known cure issue.
Labor capacity is not yet committed.

lexicon.Backup steel: A replacement source that costs more but can rescue schedule if the supplier path fails.
lexicon.Draw: A payment release against stored off-site material.
lexicon.Lot A: The first steel batch. Releasing and shipping it lets field work start.
lexicon.Lot B: The second steel batch. It must be cured and released for the full steel sequence to finish.
lexicon.Release: Inspection clearance that allows steel to ship and be installed.
lexicon.Standby: Money paid to keep crew and crane capacity available.

public_baseline.summary: PA-01 requests payment for off-site fabricated steel at tick 11.

supplier_payment_application_context.public_reason: Supplier requests off-site payment to keep cash available for documentation cure, Lot B correction, and the week 14/18 steel path.
supplier_payment_application_context.schedule_risk_if_unresolved: If cash, inspection release, and labor capacity do not line up, the first steel package can miss its reserved field-work window.
